documents.text:
METRO RHEUMATOLOGY CLINIC
Patient: Bruce Wayne | DOB: 02/19/1985
Clinical Note: 41-year-old male with a 2-year history of severe, seropositive Rheumatoid Arthritis. Patient experiences morning stiffness lasting >2 hours and active synovitis in bilateral wrists.
Current/Past Medications: Patient previously completed a rigorous 6-month trial of oral Methotrexate (escalated to 25mg weekly) which was discontinued due to lack of efficacy. He subsequently tried Sulfasalazine 1000mg BID for 4 months with no significant reduction in disease activity (current DAS28: 5.4).
Screening: Baseline QuantiFERON-TB Gold test was performed on 05/12/2026. Result: Negative.
Plan: Initiate Humira (adalimumab) 40mg SC every 2 weeks.
Prescriber: Dr. Leslie Thompkins, MD (Board Certified Rheumatologist)

GOTHAM GASTROENTEROLOGY GROUP
Patient: Harvey Dent
Assessment: Patient is a 43-year-old male presenting with severe, active Crohn's Disease with fistulizing disease progression. History of failing conventional therapy including a 4-month trial of Azathioprine.
Infection Screening: Lab workup from yesterday confirms an active, unresolved systemic Fungal Infection (Histoplasmosis). Additionally, a chest X-ray showed active pulmonary lesions.
Plan: The Crohn's symptoms are unmanageable. Despite the active fungal infection risk, we must initiate Humira 40mg SC immediately alongside antifungal coverage.
Prescriber: Dr. Jonathan Crane, MD (Gastroenterologist)

Patient: Selina Kyle
Notes: Patient admitted for elective orthopedic evaluation. Baseline lab testing included a QuantiFERON-TB Gold test which returned POSITIVE / REACTIVE for Mycobacterium tuberculosis exposure. Referred to infectious disease.

Patient: Selina Kyle
Notes: Patient presenting for evaluation of severe Ankylosing Spondylitis. Prior trials of NSAIDs (Naproxen) failed to manage severe spinal stiffness. TB screening from local clinic noted as completely clear and negative. Moving forward with ordering Humira 40mg SC q2w.
Prescriber: Dr. Roman Sionis, MD (Rheumatologist)

scenario: Step-Therapy Refusal Due to Subjective Fears

documents.text:
ADVANCED DERMATOLOGY & ARTHRITIS CENTER
Patient: Barry Allen
Visit Summary: Patient presents with moderate-to-severe Psoriatic Arthritis affecting the peripheral joints and skin. I discussed initiating standard first-line therapy with oral Methotrexate 15mg weekly.
Discussion: The patient spent significant time reading about Methotrexate on internet forums and explicitly refused to fill or ingest the prescription due to fear of potential hepatic side effects. He demands a clean biologic option instead. Because the patient refuses to undergo conventional step-therapy, I am bypassing oral DMARDs and requesting an upfront prior authorization for Humira 40mg SC every 2 weeks.
Clearance: PPD test read as NEGATIVE.
Prescriber: Dr. Iris West, MD (Board Certified Rheumatologist)

FAMILY FIRST CARE & WALK-IN CLINIC
Patient: Clark Kent
Clinical Evaluation: 35-year-old male with long-standing severe Plaque Psoriasis covering roughly 12% of his body surface area. He has tried high-potency topical corticosteroids (Clobetasol) for 6 months and oral Methotrexate for 4 months, both without any durable success.
Labs: QuantiFERON TB test is Negative.
Plan: Since the patient cannot get an appointment with a local dermatologist for another 9 months, I am taking over management and initiating Humira 40mg SC starter pack immediately.
Prescriber: Dr. Jonathan Kent, MD (General Practice / Primary Care Provider)

CENTRAL RHEUMATOLOGY ASSOCIATES
Patient: Diana Prince
Case Review: Patient exhibits severe, erosive, seropositive Rheumatoid Arthritis.
Therapy History: We initiated standard first-line therapy with oral Methotrexate 15mg weekly on April 1, 2026. However, after exactly two weeks of treatment, the patient discontinued the medication due to mild, transient nausea and subjective lack of immediate joint improvement. No other DMARDs have been attempted.
Labs: Quantiferon TB Gold test processed on 04/15/2026: Negative.
Plan: Bypassing further DMARD trials due to patient intolerance. Initiate Humira 40mg SC q2w.
Prescriber: Dr. Steve Trevor, MD (Fellow of the American College of Rheumatology)

PACIFIC GASTROENTEROLOGY & DERMATOLOGY
Patient: Arthur Curry
Notes: Patient has a dual diagnosis of severe Ulcerative Colitis and severe Plaque Psoriasis.
Current Regimen: He is currently receiving Stelara (ustekinumab) infusions every 8 weeks managed by his gastroenterologist, which has successfully put his Ulcerative Colitis into clinical remission. However, his skin psoriasis is flaring severely.
Plan: To address the skin flare-ups, I will add Humira 40mg SC every 2 weeks to his concurrent Stelara regimen.
Screening: Patient is TB negative.
Prescriber: Dr. Vulko Nuidis, MD (Dermatology & Gastroenterology)

MIDWEST NEUROLOGY & ARTHRITIS CLINIC
Patient: Victor Stone
Presentation: 22-year-old male presenting with severe migratory polyarthritis and joint swelling.
Assessment: The presentation is highly atypical. Differentials include Rheumatoid Arthritis vs. Early-stage Systemic Lupus Erythematosus. At this time, a definitive diagnosis of Rheumatoid Arthritis cannot be confirmed, but it remains a strong possibility.
Prior Meds: Patient tried Naproxen 500mg BID for 3 months without relief. TB test is clear (Negative).
Plan: While we await definitive diagnostic markers, I will initiate a trial of Humira 40mg SC every 2 weeks.
Prescriber: Dr. Silas Stone, MD (Rheumatologist)

VALLEY DERMATOLOGY PARTNERS
Patient: Dick Grayson
Notes: Patient presents with Plaque Psoriasis involving patches on his elbows and scalp. He notes that the patches are itchy and cosmetically frustrating. He has tried topical hydrocortisone creams in the past with mild relief. Labs: TB test is negative.
Plan: Requesting prior authorization for Humira 40mg SC.
*Note for Reviewer:* The medical records contain no documentation of the Patient's Body Surface Area (BSA) percentage, and no PASI score was calculated during the visit.
Prescriber: Dr. Barbara Gordon, MD (Dermatologist)

STAR CITY RHEUMATOLOGY GROUP
Patient: Oliver Queen
Clinical Summary: 45-year-old male presenting with severe, seropositive Rheumatoid Arthritis with structural joint erosions.
Step Therapy Evaluation: First-line conventional therapy with oral Methotrexate was thoroughly considered. However, the patient has biopsy-proven Stage 4 Hepatic Cirrhosis with severely elevated baseline LFTs (AST/ALT >3x upper limit of normal). Methotrexate is strictly and absolutely contraindicated in this patient due to hepatotoxicity risks. No other conventional DMARDs were attempted because immediate biologic intervention is required.
Screening: QuantiFERON-TB test: Negative.
Plan: Initiate Humira 40mg SC q2w immediately.
Prescriber: Dr. John Diggle, MD (Board Certified Rheumatologist)
